channel.title: BBC Sport
channel.copyright: Copyright: (C) British Broadcasting Corporation, see https://www.bbc.co.uk/usingthebbc/terms-of-use/#15metadataandrssfeeds for terms and conditions of reuse.

item.title: Saudi Pro League, the US or Europe - what are Salah's options?
item.description: Liverpool reporter Aadam Patel analyses what Mohamed Salah's next move might be following the forward's announcement that he is to leave the club this summer.

item.title: Liverpool & Wales great Toshack diagnosed with dementia
item.description: One of the icons of Welsh and Liverpool football has been diagnosed with dementia, his son Cameron Toshack confirms.

item.title: Lack of talent & scarred by past - Italy's 'torturous' campaign
item.description: Italy take on Northern Ireland in Thursday’s World Cup play‑off semi‑final, hoping to move one step closer to ending their lingering qualifying curse.

item.title: Rangers plan £16m share issue to raise squad funds
item.description: Rangers intend to raise £16m of new capital in a share issue to help fund investment in the playing squad, the club chairman Andrew Cavenagh has announced.

item.title: Huddersfield boss Manning takes compassionate leave
item.description: Huddersfield Town boss Liam Manning is granted compassionate leave for the remainder of the season.

item.title: £123 for a child's England kit - have prices gone too far?
item.description: BBC Sport explores the rise in the cost of kits for the 2026 World Cup, with insights from experts and fans.

item.title: Senegal appeal to be heard 'as swiftly as possible'
item.description: The Court of Arbitration for Sport (Cas) says it will rule on Senegal's appeal against being stripped of the Africa Cup of Nations title "as swiftly as possible".

item.title: Barcelona put six past Madrid in quarter-final first leg
item.description: Barcelona took control of their Women's Champions League quarter-final with an impressive, dominant away first-leg victory at Spanish rivals Real Madrid.

item.title: Madrid's Caicedo scores 'wonder strike' against Barcelona
item.description: Real Madrid's Linda Caicedo scores with a stunning effort in her team's 6-2 defeat to Barcelona in the first leg of their women's Champions League quarter-final.

item.title: Fabregas, film stars and Disney: How Como are disrupting Italian football
item.description: BBC Sport visits Como to find out how the Italian city's football club, led by coach Cesc Fabregas, is looking to break up the Serie A establishment and gatecrash the Champions League.

item.title: Russo settles thrilling Champions League first leg for Arsenal
item.description: Arsenal beat Chelsea in a thrilling Women's Champions League quarter-final first leg that featured brilliant goals from England internationals Chloe Kelly, Lauren James and Alessia Russo.

item.title: Salah's best goals for Liverpool
item.description: Watch some of the standout goals scored by Mohamed Salah for Liverpool. The Egyptian forward has revealed he will leave the club at the end of the season.

item.title: 'One of best goals I've seen' - James strikes for Chelsea
item.description: Lauren James finds the top corner with a spectacular curling effort for Chelsea against Arsenal in their Women's Champions League quarter-final.

item.title: Where does Salah rank among greatest Premier League forwards?
item.description: As Mohamed Salah announces he will leave Liverpool this summer, BBC Sport asks you to rank the best forwards in Premier League history.

item.title: Haaland donates £100,000 Viking book to home town
item.description: Erling Haaland donates a rare 16th-Century book of Viking sagas, worth £100,000, to be displayed in the library in the Norwegian town of Bryne, where he grew up.

item.title: Northern Ireland deserve Italy's respect - Buffon
item.description: World Cup winner Gianluigi Buffon says Italy have been fully concentrated on overcoming Northern Ireland as the sides prepare to face each other in Bergamo on Thursday.

item.title: No-one can replace Bale - but Wilson's giving it a go
item.description: Nobody can replace the great Gareth Bale, but Harry Wilson is stepping up more than anyone as Wales aim to qualify for the World Cup.

item.title: 'He can do everything' - Parrott carries Republic of Ireland hopes
item.description: A nation's hopes will be pinned on Troy Parrott when the Republic of Ireland face the Czech Republic in Thursday's World Cup play-off in Prague.

item.title: 'Best defeat' in Bosnia and the Wales hero you've never heard of
item.description: Wales lost their most recent game against Bosnia-Herzegovina but, thanks to a London-born Cypriot in Israel, it turned out to be the best defeat in Welsh football history.

item.title: Key questions for Clarke in Scotland's final pre-World Cup camp
item.description: What are the key questions facing Steve Clarke as he prepares Scotland for their final pre-World Cup camp?

item.title: KSI to stream Dagenham game on YouTube channel
item.description: KSI will live stream the Daggers' National League South fixture with Hampton & Richmond to his 17.8m subscribers.

item.title: NI must take advantage of Italy play-off - Magennis
item.description: Northern Ireland striker Josh Magennis says that Thursday's play-off semi-final against Italy is "a night we are going to remember for the rest of our lives."

item.title: Rodri v Zubimendi - the battle for a Spain spot
item.description: Spain have two of the world's best midfielders in Rodri and Martin Zubimendi, but can they play together?

item.title: Data usage, AI's role and player welfare - 'Football in 10 Years' discussion
item.description: Stevenage manager Alex Revell and Norwich City first-team coach Ryan Garry express their thoughts on what football could be like in 10 years' time.

item.title: Quiz: How well do you know Salah's Liverpool career?
item.description: Only the brightest will get 100% in our quiz on Mohamed Salah's time at Anfield.

item.title: Man who racially abused England defender Carter sentenced
item.description: Nigel Dewale, 60, admitted sending abusive messages over social media during the 2025 Women's Euros.

item.title: Jess Park breaks down her best goals this season
item.description: Manchester United winger Jess Park breaks down her best goals this season in the Champions League and Women's Super League.

item.title: The tie is nowhere near done - Russo
item.description: Alessia Russo reflects on her superb goal and talks about her preferred playing role following Arsenal's 3–1 victory over Chelsea in the first leg of the Women's Champions League quarter‑final.

item.title: 'What a stunner!' - Kelly doubles Arsenal's lead against Chelsea
item.description: Chloe Kelly's curling long-range strike puts Arsenal 2-0 up in the first leg of their Women's Champions League quarter-final against Chelsea.

item.title: 'An obvious error' - pundits dispute Chelsea's disallowed goal
item.description: Nikita Parris and Ellen White agree that the referee was hasty in disallowing a goal for Chelsea in the Women's Champions League quarter-final for a supposed push by Veerle Buurman on Arsenal's Laia Codina.

item.title: Bosnia 2-0 Wales - 'The best defeat of my life'
item.description: Former Wales manager Chris Coleman and midfielder Joe Ledley recall the night Wales qualified for Euro 2016, despite a 2-0 defeat in Bosnia-Herzegovina.

item.title: How a coach helped Chelsea & Azpilicueta sleep for success
item.description: Former Chelsea captain Cesar Azpilicueta is one of the club's modern-greats, with part of his success coming after working on his sleep.

item.title: Republic of Ireland not dwelling on past hurt - Coleman
item.description: Republic of Ireland stalwart Seamus Coleman says he is confident the squad are not carrying the pain of the country's failed qualifying bids before Thursday's World Cup play-off semi-final against the Czech Republic.

item.title: 'It wasn't meant to end like this' - Salah exit tough but inevitable
item.description: Less than a year ago, Mohamed Salah was sitting on a throne inside Anfield after renewing his contract - so why is he leaving?

item.title: Numbers behind 'the most polluting World Cup'
item.description: BBC Sport analyses the environmental cost of a 48-team World Cup held across North America.

item.title: Tonali prefers Italy return - Wednesday's gossip
item.description: Newcastle midfielder Sandro Tonali wants Italy return despite interest from Manchester rivals, Ben White may be on his way out at Arsenal and Bournemouth boss Andoni Iraola eyed by Premier League rivals.

item.title: Does running more in a game actually make a difference?
item.description: Tottenham significantly outran Nottingham Forest on Sunday but still ended up losing 3-0, so does running more make a difference?

item.title: Morecambe served with winding-up petition
item.description: A winding-up petition has been filed against Morecambe Football Club, the National League side have confirmed.

item.title: Liverpool must find someone to hit Salah numbers - Warnock
item.description: Former Liverpool defender and Match of the Day pundit Stephen Warnock reacts to Mohamed Salah's announcement that he will leave Liverpool at the end of this season.

item.title: Arsenal's Smith v Chelsea's Thompson in Mastermind quiz
item.description: Chelsea's Alyssa Thompson and Arsenal's Olivia Smith go head-to-head in a Mastermind quiz, choosing Los Angeles and Harry Potter as their specialist subjects.

item.title: Should Raya have started over Arrizabalaga in final?
item.description: The Monday Night Club's Rory Smith and Chris Sutton debate whether Arsenal manager Mikel Arteta made the correct decision in starting Kepa Arrizabalaga over David Raya in the Carabao Cup final.

item.title: Tuchel clearly doesn't rate Alexander-Arnold - Sutton
item.description: The Monday Night Club's Chris Sutton and Rory Smith discuss Thomas Tuchel's decision to exclude Trent Alexander-Arnold from the latest England squad.

item.title: 'Capitulation' - how 'fragile' Tottenham put Tudor under pressure
item.description: Kelly Cates, Joe Hart and Danny Murphy discuss how a "fragile" Tottenham "fell apart" against Nottingham Forest, and what this could mean for interim boss Igor Tudor's position.

item.title: Football's great entertainers - ranking the biggest showboaters
item.description: We all love to watch the players with the flicks and tricks, the mavericks with the magic manoeuvres. Here, BBC Sport ranks football's top showboaters.

item.title: Scots playing with pressure encourages Andreatta
item.description: Having members of the Scotland squad "playing with high pressure" for their clubs will benefit the national side's quest for the World Cup, says head coach Melissa Andreatta.

item.title: Sexism in football - a problem that isn't going away
item.description: BBC Sport speaks to fans, the police and others to examine how common sexism is in football, why it happens, and what can be done.

item.title: History beckons as Glasgow City and Rangers meet again
item.description: Glasgow City and Rangers bid to keep their treble ambitions on course in Sunday's SWPL Cup final.

item.title: Who can still qualify for the World Cup?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team explains which countries can still qualify for the World Cup via the European play-offs and intercontinental play-offs.

item.title: Have Newcastle gone backwards?
item.description: Match of the Day's Kelly Cates, Joe Hart and Danny Murphy discuss how Newcastle are "struggling with consistency" since competing in the Champions League.

item.title: How 'confident' McGinn makes the difference for Villa
item.description: Kelly Cates, Joe Hart, Danny Murphy discuss the impact of midfielder John McGinn's "confidence" and "creativity" for Aston Villa in his side's 2-0 victory against West Ham.

item.title: How 'all-action' Anderson inspired Forest win
item.description: Kelly Cates, Joe Hart and Danny Murphy discuss why Nottingham Forest midfielder Elliot Anderson is "the favourite" to be in the starting XI for England in this summer's World Cup.

item.title: Wilson's Fulham form 'no surprise to Wales' - Ampadu
item.description: Ethan Ampadu says Harry Wilson’s outstanding Fulham form comes as no surprise to those inside the Wales camp.

item.title: 'Man City would be stupid to let Shaw go!'
item.description: BBC Sport pundits Fara Williams and Nia Jones explain why Bunny Shaw is the best forward in the game right now.

item.title: How Scotland reached the World Cup - told by those who made it happen
item.description: Tom English speaks to those who returned Scotland to the men's World Cup finals for the first time since 1998 about the epic win over Denmark.

item.title: 'Northern Ireland have nothing to fear but everything to gain'
item.description: In a column for BBC Sport NI, Stuart Dallas says Northern Ireland "have nothing to fear but everything to gain" in their World Cup play-off in Italy.

item.title: Is the three-week Premier League break good for your side?
item.description: The Premier League is on a break for almost three weeks as international football takes centre stage, but will it give your club a chance to reset or disrupt momentum?

item.title: Dirt pitches, dodging dogs and choosing Chelsea - the making of Estevao
item.description: From the dirt pitch to practising against a rottweiler and Thiago Silva's advice - the foundations of Chelsea forward Estevao's rise.

item.title: Are autograph hunters going too far?
item.description: BBC Sport explores how footballers and managers feel about professional autograph hunters, after a series of controversial incidents.

item.title: Four hours inside Bellamy's office - and the Wales boss' complex mind
item.description: Wales boss Craig Bellamy discusses everything from football to Balkan history - and playing Ken and Barbie - in an exclusive, in-depth interview with BBC Sport.

item.title: Taking wildcards, leaving out stars - the art of World Cup squad building
item.description: Managers often get it wrong when it comes to picking World Cup squads - is one packed with stars always the best option?

item.title: All February and March's done deals, including deadline day
item.description: Check out the significant signings and departures in the Premier League, Scottish Premiership, EFL and Women's Super League.

item.title: Who is one card away from suspension in the Premier League?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team looks at who is currently nearing a suspension in the Premier League.

item.title: All you need to know about 2027 Women's World Cup
item.description: Our Ask Me Anything team take a look at everything you need to know for the 2027 Women's World Cup.

item.title: When can Arsenal win the Premier League?
item.description: Arsenal are nine points clear at the top of the Premier League, but when can they officially win the title?

item.title: What happens when a manager gets suspended?
item.description: Ever wondered what a touchline ban entails? BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team explain what punishment managers actually face.

item.title: Which club has the most red cards in a Premier League season?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything looks at the record for most red cards in a Premier League season

item.title: VAR vs TMO – what are the key differences?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything looks at the key differences between football's video assistant referees and rugby union's use of television match officials.

item.title: Who has the most Premier League appearances?
item.description: Our Ask Me Anything Team look at which players have the highest total Premier League appearances.

item.title: Engels 'never made issue' after Forest interest - gossip
item.description: Celtic boss on midfielder after move south failed to materialise and Hibernian head coach on rivals creating "more noise".

item.title: Dunfermline footballer 'heartbroken' after death of partner
item.description: Rachel Borthwick, 36, was first diagnosed with breast cancer in 2021.

item.title: Why ex-Palace winger Olise is now in Ballon d'Or contention at Bayern
item.description: Michael Olise has been in superb form for Bayern Munich this season, has the French winger put himself in contention for the Ballon d'Or?

item.title: Laporta re-elected Barcelona president for fourth term
item.description: Joan Laporta vows Barcelona will be "unstoppable" as he is re-elected club president for a further five years.

item.title: From Bodo to Brentford - ranking this season's biggest overachievers
item.description: Bodo/Glimt must be the most romantic story in European football in decades but are they the team that has defied the odds the most this season? Here are all 10 contenders...

item.title: Cole appointed manager of Serie B side Cesena
item.description: Former Arsenal and Chelsea defender Ashley Cole appointed manager of Serie B side Cesena until the end of the season.

item.title: Women's game needs more respect with officiating - Bompastor
item.description: Chelsea manager Sonia Bompastor says the women's game needs "more respect" after her side have a goal controversially disallowed in their Women's Champions League quarter-final first leg against Arsenal.

item.title: Frank's downfall: Inside his struggle to get players and fans onboard
item.description: Thomas Frank has been sacked as Tottenham manager after just eight months in charge. BBC Sport's Sami Mokbel takes a closer look at his tenure.

item.title: Cages, crushes and stabbings - is European away safety getting worse?
item.description: BBC Sport explores the safety issues fans face when following their team across Europe.

item.title: The great number nine decline - where have England's strikers gone?
item.description: BBC Sport takes an in-depth look at why England lack centre-forward options behind Harry Kane - a far cry from when the likes of Les Ferdinand only won 17 caps.

item.title: Captain, player, manager, Mister, number one - Davids at Barnet
item.description: BBC Sport remembers Edgar Davids' 15 months as Barnet player-manager in League Two and the Conference.

item.title: From farming village to Liverpool icon - the Salah story
item.description: BBC Sport visits Egypt to discover what Mohamed Salah means to the people of the football-mad country, and how a small boy from humble beginnings became a national icon.

item.title: Napoli 'smell blood' & target Premier League's discarded stars
item.description: After Scott McTominay was a hit, Napoli went back to the Premier League for Kevin de Bruyne, could more players follow them to Serie A?

item.title: When River Plate icon Diaz took over fourth-tier Oxford
item.description: Twenty years on from Ramon Diaz's surprising appointment at Oxford United, BBC Sport looks at what happened and how it went.
